latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
14 May 2026 15:31
RNS Number : 4050E
Ceres Power Holdings plc
14 May 2026
CWR.L
14 May 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres Power" or "the Company")
Directorate change
Horsham, UK:
In accordance with LR 6.4.6R (2), as previously reported Ceres Power announces that Trine Borum Bojsen completed her term as a Non-executive Director of the Company at the close of the AGM held 14 May 2026.
As stated in the Annual Report and Accounts of the Company for the year ended 31 December 2025, Warren Finegold, Ceres Power Holdings plc Group Chair, said:
"In view of her increased operating responsibilities at Equinor, where she has been promoted to Senior Vice President PWR Global Offshore Wind, Trine decided not to stand for re-election at the AGM. We congratulate Trine on her promotion and thank her for her many important contributions to Ceres over the last four years."
For further information visit
www.ceres.tech
or contact:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Merryl Black (Head of Investor Relations and Sustainability)
Email: investors@cerespower.com
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Dominic Murray (Company Secretary)
Tel: +44 (0)7526 196529
About Ceres
Ceres is a leading developer of clean energy technology: fuel cells for power generation and electrolysers to produce green hydrogen. Its asset-light, licensing model has seen it establish partnerships with some of the world's largest companies, such as Doosan, Delta, Denso, Shell, Weichai and Thermax. Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy.
Read more on our website
www.ceres.tech
or follow us on
LinkedIn
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact

latest.title: Grant of Share Options under the Sharesave Scheme
latest.content:
Today 15:30
RNS Number : 6615D
Ceres Power Holdings plc
08 May 2026
CWR.L
08 May 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres Power" or "the Company")
Grant of Share Options under the Sharesave Scheme
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology, confirms the grant of options on 8 May 2026 under the Ceres Power Holdings plc Sharesave Scheme (the "Scheme"). The Scheme is an 'all employee share scheme' which is available annually to all employees subject to qualifying conditions, which was approved by the HM Revenue & Customs, and which was introduced to encourage wider employee share ownership of the Company.
Options to purchase a total of 376,277 ordinary shares of 10 pence of the Company ("Ordinary Shares") have been granted. These options will be exercisable between 1 June 2029 and 30 November 2029 at an exercise price of £2.4352 per share.
The following grants were made to Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ("PDMRs"):

company_30s.what_they_do: CWR is a niche technology firm that builds software platforms for monitoring and managing energy infrastructure. Its products help utilities and industrial operators track asset health, predict failures, and optimize maintenance schedules.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The energy sector is rapidly adopting digital solutions to reduce downtime and cut operating costs. CWR’s platform sits at the intersection of IoT, data analytics, and asset management, offering a scalable way for utilities to extend asset life and improve reliability. In a market where regulatory pressure for smarter grids is mounting, a solid digital tool can become a critical operating component.
company_30s.current_state: The company is currently in a restructuring phase. Recent director changes and a partnership announcement with Delta Electronics and Centrica signal a pivot toward more stable, partnership‑driven revenue. The stock is trading at a very low price point, reflecting a steep drop in market confidence but also creating a deep bottom that could be primed for a rebound.

rns_narrative: CWR has been busy reshaping its leadership and governance structure, with several director appointments and a change of adviser announced in mid‑May. This suggests the board is tightening control and preparing for a new strategic direction.

A key partnership with Delta Electronics and Centrica has been announced, positioning CWR to deliver joint solutions in the smart‑grid space. This collaboration could unlock new customer contracts and provide a steady revenue stream.

Shareholder activity – including share‑option grants and changes in director holdings – indicates that insiders are aligning their interests with the company’s long‑term plan, a signal that management believes the business can rebound.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – very few active posts, but a handful of bullish voices highlight the partnership as a turning point.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of traders praise the Delta‑Centrica deal, saying it could bring in high‑margin contracts. One post even claims the partnership could push the share price above the 52‑week high if the pilots succeed.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some skeptics worry that the partnership is too vague and that CWR’s product line lacks differentiation. A few posts cite the company’s history of low earnings and question whether the new deals will materialize before the next quarter.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood is shifting from neutral to mildly bullish after the partnership announcement. However, overall engagement remains low, so the community is largely silent.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search activity for CWR, giving the stock a high surprise potential if any catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The first public report of a pilot with Centrica – whether a case study or a signed contract – could act as a clear sign that the partnership is moving beyond paperwork.
Release of Q3 earnings that beat revenue expectations would validate the new business model and likely trigger a price rally.
Any regulatory approval or certification for the joint smart‑grid solution would remove a key barrier to commercial deployment.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike on the day of the partnership announcement, a positive earnings surprise, or a press release announcing a signed customer contract. A breakout above the most recent 20‑day high with sustained volume would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is in the early recovery stage of a deep crash. With a tight APEX score, the market is primed for a quick bounce once a catalyst confirms that the partnership is moving forward. Patience is required until the first tangible evidence of revenue growth appears.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock sits at a deep bottom with very low public interest – a classic setup for a contrarian bounce. If the partnership delivers even modest revenue in the next quarter, the price could double or triple as the market re‑prices the company’s future prospects. A recovery to 50% of the 52‑week high would represent a 200–300% upside relative to the current trading level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The partnership remains unproven; if the pilot fails or the contract is delayed, the stock could stay in a low‑range trap. The company’s past earnings volatility and lack of clear revenue streams also mean that a failure to deliver could push the price back down to the current low. The worst‑case scenario is a 70% decline from the current trading level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 200–300% versus a downside of 70% gives an asymmetry of roughly 3:1. The risk is limited to the current price level, while the reward hinges on a successful partnership rollout.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the latest 20‑day high with a stop 10–15% below that level. Use a position size that caps loss to a small percentage of capital, given the high upside potential. Trail the stop as the price climbs to protect gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a volume surge on the partnership announcement or a positive earnings report. A clear signal of revenue growth is the trigger to go long.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risk identified; therefore, the setup remains bullish until evidence of bankruptcy, delisting, or a catastrophic partnership collapse emerges.

ai_insights.bottom_line: CWR’s deep crash, zero public chatter, and a fresh partnership with industry leaders create a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian opportunity that will likely materialise once the partnership shows tangible progress.
